pattern: Tallying only the even values and then breaking ties by smallest key turns a frequency question into a single pass plus a deterministic selection.
hints: You only care about even numbers; odd ones never matter to the answer.
Count how often each even value occurs, then decide which one wins.
When two even values share the highest count, the smaller value should be preferred; if no even value exists at all, the answer is -1.

edge_inputs.description: tie between two even values resolved by smaller
no even element at all returns -1
zero counts as even and ties on count with another even
largest value but not most frequent

stress.description: many repeats over a small even-heavy range to force ties
